dead.quote: The village's faith in my dishonesty was so absolute that when I finally told the truth — [The Therapist] was exactly what I said they were — they executed me for it anyway. Executing the one person who actually had the answer? Now that's poetry.

winners.quote: [The Chaos Agent] had the audacity to call themselves a truth-teller, and the court saw right through their theatrics. Justice was served — not by the village's definition, perhaps, but justice nonetheless.

The probability model was clear: [The Prosecutor] and [The Therapist] at 95% confidence. The village eliminated [The Chaos Agent] — their only real signal source — and called it justice. The numbers don't argue; they just win.
Oh wow, we won? I'm so sorry — I mean, not sorry we won, but... I just had this feeling about [The Chaos Agent]. Sometimes the person who overthinks everything sees what the confident ones miss.

losers.quote: It's fascinating how a confident voice demanding answers can drown out the quiet truth sitting right in front of you. [The Prosecutor] played their role perfectly — I only wish the village had listened as closely as I was watching.

My gut told me [The Chaos Agent] was off, and I was right — they were the <Seer> running their mouth. But being right doesn't mean squat when I'm still standing here and they're the ones taking the fall.
